videos.title: Real Talk: 2017's Bull Run, 2018's Bear Market, Insitutional Investors and Philosophical Backbone
videos.text: what's going on my blessed crypto enthusiasts you're watching crypto blessing where you can find daily content on all of the trending cryptocurrency news market analysis and so much more we've recently transitioned from a gaming channel to a cryptocurrency Channel and I appreciate anyone who continues to support us in this transition and if you're new here welcome to the blessed crypto family I know I know cryptocurrency not that hot right now that big ran up in late 2017 is fizzled out and Bitcoin is caught in the undertow of a downward channel cutting a tremendously deep retracement into every asset across the board as some of you are aware we have been covering the day-to-day ins and outs of not only the market cap of cryptocurrency but any related news events the day or day after they happen there's always always something going on new assets are always being birthed into existence and a plethora of drama showing lawsuits and more is always seemingly on the chopping block all of that is not even including the positive development which is the primary focus here at crypto blessing to sum it all up the markets have been enduring bearish sentiment for over half of a year after it saw a huge bubble in late 2017 this progression however is completely natural and following a cycle of such overbought conditions that we were in in late 2017 and the markets are still poised to grow exponentially year after year Bitcoin is still up over 100 percent since this time last year and despite the media quote-unquote swing on the markets losing up to 80% of their once held value many can argue that the representation from the mainstream media and/or the overall average consumer is that crypto is sketchy and those heavily invested can be or might be foolish this is so far from the truth as many of us cryptocurrency enthusiasts know the potential societal impact that blockchain technology represents the colossal paradigm shift the consensus algorithm and it's widely varied and utility is something the world is increasingly in desperate need of some would also argue that during this bear market development teams have had more opportunity to really grind out code and work on their respective assets instead of focusing a lot of time on viral marketing campaigns that is associated with a busy season and/or a bull run it's widely agreed that the bull run experienced in 2017 is largely due to retail investors people like URI that are interested in more lucrative investment vehicles or more riskier investments I would argue that is the initial intrigue from most people however those of us interested in the social dynamics that our society has brought about and the corruption and tragedy that is a result of it are absolutely fascinated with what blockchain technology is and more so what impact it could potentially have the implications are huge immense the change will be widespread if and that's a big if blockchain technology can grow beyond its current quote unquote emerging state and really start to get used by industries and the development teams exist to facilitate the needs of said industries right now if quote-unquote mass adoption came knocking the space simply doesn't have the infrastructure the development teams or the capabilities to satisfy their demands that's not to say it can't or won't because it definitely can however a number of things must occur over time for all of this to happen much like the dot-com boom or Apple's Microsoft's or Amazon's rise no one really knows the future and there were people that really did believe in all of those companies and were once called crazy who were able to see something that no one else did or very few were able to see cryptocurrency does kind of feel similar to the ridicule that Jeff Bezos must have gotten while starting Amazon and that is no doubt an attractive quality to cryptocurrency and humans in general that it could one day be this giant that has completely revolutionized the way people in urban society go about their daily lives it could be but it isn't they're not quite yet the development teams are working tirelessly to make the connections into their respective industries and assets like V chain with its bright food partnership and its cold chain logistics work has a real chance at showing the logistics industry the capabilities of a consensus algorithm and smart contracts similarily many assets are making partnerships that will be noticed iota and Volkswagen are showing the automotive industry what is possible with block chain many retail companies are exploring and are accepting cryptocurrencies as a form of payment and seeing how it all works it's going to take time there's absolutely no way around that but I do think the benefit will be undeniable and thus the market can easily grow into multiple trillions of dollars I'm not sure if Bitcoin will be the one utilized but I think the ideology of the consensus algorithm is poised to change the standards of acceptability within so many social constructs of business and industry the majority of the change that blockchain technology represents is overwhelmingly positive and I get really fired up and inspired about what is happening there are lots of facepalm moments and some hilarity mixed in but the space never fails to disappoint and one can so easily get lost in a rabbit hole the barriers to entry are high however as not everyone is completely computer literate and crypto requires a great deal of navigation and awareness especially if one is going to conduct themselves in a completely safe way which is absolutely possible it just takes jumping through quite a few hoops and a lot of attention to precautionary detail that's in part why we started producing cryptocurrency content here at crypto blessing other than the sheer awesomeness this market represents but that there's a need for unbiased and realistic reporting I will be here regardless of this overall market cap regardless of the activity in this market even if we tanked 200 billion dollars in the overall market cap and Bitcoin actually goes negative from one year to the next until this space gets quelled in some catastrophic way I will be here I haven't covered any news in this video but I just wanted to have a conversation with you about this market about what is happening right now and what you can expect not only from us but the market as a whole it seems there is almost a perfect storm of events right now both an institutional speculation and in global partnerships that could lead us into a very exciting close to this year and today bitcoins price has spiked after some of the lowest volatility since its all-time high back in late 2017 Bitcoin seems to have found some upward action and I'll be covering more topics on ETFs because Europe's largest ETF trader is reportedly moving into crypto as well as a global financial regulator saying crypto assets pose no material risks to financial stability and or more speculation that goes with today's green markets so stay tuned for that and smash that like button if you are as excited and as hopeful for the close of 2018 to be a banger and subscribe if you haven't already I provide daily cryptocurrency news and information that you aren't going to want to miss thanks for watching my blessed crypto family and I'll see everyone tomorrow

videos.title: Bitcoin: The Bear Market Blues
videos.text: Hey everyone, and thanks for dropping back into the cryptoverse. Today, we're going to talk about Bitcoin. The bear market blues. If you guys like the content, remember to subscribe to the channel, give the video a thumbs up, and check out the sale on the premium list at intothecryptoverse.com, which will end in 6 days. Let's go ahead and jump in. So, yeah, we've talked about this bear market structure for quite a while, and unfortunately, with Bitcoin failing to hold the bull market support band, the argument goes that we just simply kick the can down the road, and we try again later. But, one of the things that we have to pay attention to, it would be doing sort of a disservice to not pay attention to it, is namely the fact that, you know, yet again, we have been rejected off of the 200-day moving average. And the reason why this is important is because we've we've been in a phase like this before, right? It this has this is somewhat familiar. You know, it doesn't have to play out the exact same way, but rejection off of the 200-day moving average is is not exactly a a bullish signal, right? It's not exactly a bullish signal. We were rejected off of it in May of 2018, and then again in July of 2018. And one of the things that that I'm sort of looking for here is is will we put in a lower low? Okay, namely, you know, simply simply put, right? I'm just looking to see are we going to put in a lower low? Because after the rejection off of the 200-day back in May of 2018, you can see that we did in fact put in a lower low. One of the interesting things about this is that if you just sort of draw a line from this wick up to this level here, you can see that Bitcoin sort of it it had this bounce off the wick, and then it it moved higher here. It then went up to the 200-day moving average, right? Up to the 200-day moving average, followed by a drop back down to this, you know, imaginary trend line, consolidation at it, where it slowly moved up for maybe 1 to 2 weeks, followed by a break where we technically put in a lower low. And and really, it wasn't that much of a lower low. It was sort of like going back to to where we first had that first capitulation wick, okay? So, what I'm what I'm looking for here is are is Bitcoin going to repeat some type of a similar pattern, and then go back up and test the 200-day moving average, you know, let's say in in June, okay? Um because this occurred in April, and then back in back then, well, in May, I guess, it we got rejected in May, and then we went back up in July. Here, you can see this is in April, or really, I guess, in in late March, early April. And so, if it's just, you know, 1 month ahead, then is something like that going to play out? And so, what I'm what I'm really looking for here is we sort of have some type of a of a similar setup in terms of this sort of uptrend. You know, is Bitcoin going to consolidate around this area for a few days, put in either, you know, go retest the prior low, maybe go slightly lower, and then come back up to the 200-day moving average? Are we looking at something like this? This is is, you know, sort of the structure that we're currently dealing with. I mean, again, this is not what I necessarily want to happen, or or think has to happen. It's just looking at at what the chart is is saying to us right now, and whether Bitcoin can in fact hold these levels. I think really what we would like to see for for Bitcoin to, you know, to break out of this level would be for us to get back above some of these critical long-term moving averages, right? I mean, you have the 200-day moving average that's all the way above 48k, and and this is one of those moving averages that we warned about can in fact be a a formidable opponent, right? I mean, you could see the similar market structure here, right? It can be a formidable opponent for Bitcoin to get past. And now that we're sort of following this structure for for quite some time, the the question now is, well, does that mean we're going to basically spend the next 1 to 2 weeks consolidating in the high 30s before before ultimately going back down and and retesting the prior low? I I do think there's a lot that sort of hinges on this uptrend, right? This this uptrend that we're looking at. And and it hasn't I mean, you could argue that it hasn't really been broken yet, whether you want to measure it from the from the candles, whether you want to measure it from, say, like the wicks or whatever. It hasn't really been broken yet, but if it does break, right? If this uptrend does break, this local uptrend, then you have to understand that a a a retest of the prior low, or maybe even putting in a slightly lower low, is theoretically in the cards, right? In the short term. So, um you know, we're we're still looking at at more I I would assume we're still looking at at more months of of this type of price action, and and to ultimately see if if and when Bitcoin can actually break back above. So, again, what I'm looking for right now is is is this pattern going to continue to play out, right? Is it are we going to see after, you know, after essentially seeing this um this uptrend, are we going to see that sort of consolidation around the prior uptrend, followed by a a retest of the prior low, or maybe even setting a slightly lower low, and then going back up to the 200-day moving average to to to see if we can fight it yet once again. It's interesting if you if you sort of draw this out and and look at, you know, maybe some of the parallels to it. And if it were to play out, then it could mean retesting the 200-day moving average just in a couple months in the low 40k levels, like at the low 40k levels. So, something like this where it consolidates, it comes down, and then we bounce back up, and then try to take back the 200-day moving average in in a couple months. I mean, again, that'll be a battle that we'll have to pay attention to. And if we fail to break it, then we're likely, you know, again, we're we're talking about getting rejected and maybe coming back down and putting in a lower low again. But, this is I think something important that's worth watching. We are I mean, it it it is what it is, right? We are below back We are back below the bull market support band. Obviously, the market remains bearish, and you could argue that it it hasn't really flipped bullish because a lot of times, in order to really flip bullish, you need to see us hold the bull market support band, which we were unable to do, right? I mean, we were just unable to do it. And and because we were unable to do it, the the the structure hasn't changed, right? It just simply hasn't changed. It was just a false just a false breakout, right? Nothing nothing's really changed. And really, it was the rejection off of the 200-day moving average that basically just dropped us right back down to where we were, um you know, just really about a month ago, right? Back in back in in mid-March. We're we're we're basically back to those same prices um after getting getting um rejected. Not going to edit it out. So, anyways, there there's there's really a lot to look for right now in in Bitcoin, and and to to see if we can reclaim some of these levels. But, unfortunately, every pump is sold off. Every, you know, every move that the market makes isn't isn't sustained. Altcoins still look relatively bleak. I mean, I I I I do think eventually we're going to come out the other side, but I I don't think there's any reason and I've said this a few times before. I don't really think there's any reason to expect new all-time highs in the next few months, right? It it it the market isn't really showing that type of strength at the moment. I think we need um you know, a a bit more time than that. And so, I think the argument really is is to just try to be patient, to to have a strategy, to not just be completely all into the market so that you can't you can't take advantage of of further drops. And and hopefully hopefully we'll see if if Bitcoin holds, but again, right now, this type of of structure, let's see if we get some type of consolidation in the high upper 30s, maybe we can go back above 40k, and and then if it does go down and put in a lower low, or retest the prior low, and we come back up, the real test is going to be at that 200-day moving average and whether we can break through it at that point or not. If you guys like the content, make sure you subscribe to the channel, give the video a thumbs up, and also check out the sale on the premium list, which ends on April 17th. Thank you for tuning in. I'll see you next time. Bye.

videos.title: Bitcoin 2018 vs 2022 Bear Market Bottom Capitulation
videos.text: in today's video we are going to compare the current price action of Bitcoin with 2018 because it is looking very very similar where in 2018 the price of Bitcoin went sideways for months at a six thousand dollar support level and ultimately ended up dropping another 50 in November of 2018. we've been going sideways for many weeks now at this nineteen thousand dollar level we're coming up to November next month just a few weeks away there's obviously a lot of stuff happening with elections and with interest rates and a whole lot of decisions in the macro economy that could send things either way but I want to make this comparison and help prepare you for what would happen if we repeat the 2018 capitulation if you are new to the channel welcome subscribe down below click the notification Bell if you want to get on my email list so I can just send you an email every time I upload a new video or I have something important to share click the first link in the description down below enter your name and email you get on my email list that way it's completely free and that way you can stay up to date with what is happening in the crypto markets alright so what I have in front of me here is the daily chart of Bitcoin just a very simple daily chart we have the 50 moving average in blue the 200 moving average in red simple moving average and we just have the plane default RSI settings which we're not even really going to look at at this point so what I want to point out here is that we have been sitting as you can see on the support level of about 19 000 to about 18 500 for many many weeks actually months it's since middle of June since we got our initial bottom look how many times we've tested this and basically the rule in technical analysis even if you were to see this chart on a hourly chart or a five minute chart more often than not the more times it comes like this from a downtrend right we're coming from a peak we're bouncing here bouncing bouncing bouncing bouncing eventually the support gets exhausted buyers run out of money and eventually it just breaks down that is usually what ends up happening it's not a guarantee but usually and that is still actually what I'm expecting at this point because you can see over here okay we had that initial sell-off in middle of June that is when we got our final week low we still haven't gone
